introSection: Raging Bull is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Six Flags Great America in Gurnee, Illinois, USA. The ride opened on May 1, 1999 as the second Bolliger & Mabillard roller coaster to exceed. Like Viper, Raging Bull is located in Southwest Territory, a frontier-themed area.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Six Flags Great America announced the addition of Raging Bull in October 1998. It was part of a $25 million investment into the park for 1999. Construction of Raging Bull started in November of that year. Raging Bull opened on May 1, 1999.

During the 2008-2009 off-season, the sign for the coaster was replaced and the ride was repainted.

On June 15, 2016, Six Flags announced that virtual reality would be put on the ride.

On August 11, 2025, Six Flags Great America announced that Raging Bull would temporarily close starting August 18 in order for a new repaint job. It reopened on October 3.

Before the park opened for the 2026 season, the park announced at the No Coaster Con event held by American Coaster Enthusiasts that the ride's former queue staircase to the station would be replaced.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Raging Bull is one of two Bolliger & Mabillard hyper roller coasters to feature a pre drop, with the other one being Apollo's Chariot at Busch Gardens Williamsburg. Unlike other Bolliger & Mabillard coasters, the coaster has a "Hyper-Twister" layout instead of the "Out and Back" layout hyper coasters usually star. The layout was different so that the ride could fit in a relatively small plot of land. It is themed to a ferocious beast that terrorized residents of the old Southwest Territory until they fled the town.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Raging Bull has an orange track and maroon supports.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On May 3, 2003, the park's first day of the season, an 11-year-old girl died while choking on a taffy during the ride.

fullText:
Raging Bull is a steel hyper roller coaster located at Six Flags Great America in Gurnee, Illinois, USA. The ride opened on May 1, 1999 as the second Bolliger & Mabillard roller coaster to exceed. Like Viper, Raging Bull is located in Southwest Territory, a frontier-themed area.

Six Flags Great America announced the addition of Raging Bull in October 1998. It was part of a $25 million investment into the park for 1999. Construction of Raging Bull started in November of that year. Raging Bull opened on May 1, 1999.

During the 2008-2009 off-season, the sign for the coaster was replaced and the ride was repainted.

On June 15, 2016, Six Flags announced that virtual reality would be put on the ride.

On August 11, 2025, Six Flags Great America announced that Raging Bull would temporarily close starting August 18 in order for a new repaint job. It reopened on October 3.

Before the park opened for the 2026 season, the park announced at the No Coaster Con event held by American Coaster Enthusiasts that the ride's former queue staircase to the station would be replaced.

Raging Bull is one of two Bolliger & Mabillard hyper roller coasters to feature a pre drop, with the other one being Apollo's Chariot at Busch Gardens Williamsburg. Unlike other Bolliger & Mabillard coasters, the coaster has a "Hyper-Twister" layout instead of the "Out and Back" layout hyper coasters usually star. The layout was different so that the ride could fit in a relatively small plot of land. It is themed to a ferocious beast that terrorized residents of the old Southwest Territory until they fled the town.

Raging Bull has an orange track and maroon supports.



On May 3, 2003, the park's first day of the season, an 11-year-old girl died while choking on a taffy during the ride.
